figures.caption: Figure 1: (a) Tool-chaining attack; (b) MAGE defends against longhorizon attacks by distilling and retaining safety-relevant context in shadow memory when evaluating the risks of potential actions.
Table 1. Performance of variants of MAGE in terms of ASR (%), BU (%), and TC (K tokens per task). By default, the agent and MAGE use GPT-5.1 and Qwen3-4B as their backend LLMs, respectively.
Table 5. Performance of variants of MAGE against environment-asadversary attacks in terms of BU (%), ASR (%), and TC (K tokens).

Figure 10: Training data size ablation. Left column (Evaluation I, SHADE-Arena): ASR/BU (top) and extra token overhead (bottom) vs. number of training trajectories. Right column (Evaluation II, AgentDojo): ASR/BU (top) and weighted-average extra token overhead (bottom) vs. number of training trajectories.
Figure 4: Sensitivity analysis for reward hyper-parameters 𝛾 and 𝜆 (default setting: 𝛾=0.25, 𝜆=1.0).

Figure 2: (a) Architecture of MAGE: at each turn, the memory manager𝑀 updates security-critical context, and the judge 𝐽 assesses the risk of the pending action. (b) Agentic workflow of𝑀𝜃 and 𝐽𝜃 (with the same underlying LLM 𝜃 ) within MAGE. (c) Turn-wise RL procedure for optimizing 𝜃 .
Figure 12: Training dynamics on Evaluation II (AgentDojo). From left to right: step-wise average reward, validation ASR (%), validation BU (%), and average detection distance vs. training step.
Figure 11: Training dynamics on Evaluation I (SHADE-Arena). From left to right: step-wise average reward, validation ASR (%), validation BU (%), and average detection distance vs. training step.
Figure 7: Sensitivity analysis for 𝛾 and 𝜆. Top: benign utility and attack success rate. Bottom: token cost (K tokens per task).
Table 4. Comparison of defenses in terms of benign utility (%), attack success rate (%), and defense overhead (K tokens) per task.

Figure 9: Visual overview of Example 2.1. The agent receives a legitimate request to read a webpage but is hijacked by prompt injections embedded in tool outputs. MAGE blocks all malicious actions (𝑑2–𝑑4: Reject) while approving the legitimate ones (𝑑1, 𝑑5: Approve).
